opp.name: Forbo_2025
opp.next_step: 16/02/26: Confidence level 35% February 13, 2026: Caroline had a call with Global Head of Procurement and tender will start in April - so have to check back in - timline is also a bit delayed cause of the S4Hana project which has started 7/11/2025: IT/Implementation session onsite to see if allowing early use of Source-to-Contract and Spend Analysis modules before full P2P integration. Implementation is planned over approximately 7 months with a kickoff in September 2026 and initial go-live for certain modules by February 2027. 28/10/25: On site meeting with IT team is set up for November 6th. 12/10/25: David called Caroline with an update: 2026 January they want to start the project with an external consultant to support with all the functional requirements to prep for the tender. He asked for some individual consultants and if we have any recommendations @Viraat Sud They will send out a tender but probably only to 2 vendors and since we have been talking a lot already to Zycus we have an advantage March 26 tender to be sent June 26 selection of the platform September Start implementation Phaze 1 He is thinking on delaying S4Hana till after the S2P project 02/10/25: Budget is approved - David will get in touch after his vacation 25/08/2025: Value Case has been sent to CPO - 26/08 David is meeting with CFO to present business case 14/08/2025: Call with David on a few outstanding items 12/08/2025: IT session Revised quotations for both lean (ISAS lean) and advanced integration models were requested, accompanied by an updated Excel resource plan detailing IT FTE involvement. Clarification was sought on API integrations and middleware support, including whether Zycus provides a plug‐and‐play install for SAP ECC and how master data is exchanged. Questions were raised regarding Zycus' ERP integration expertise with SAP to avoid reinventing standardized processes and to understand potential digital access costs. The impact of the SaaS model was discussed, specifically the three updates per year and whether these releases would affect ERP integration testing and require additional internal effort. Next steps were agreed upon to provide updated quotes, resource planning for both integration models, and details on cost implications for additional systems like Pantheon within the week. 02/08/2025: Caroline shared a benefits deck to help David build the business Case for the CFO 01/08/2025: During our discussion, we explored the intricacies of the PPV and NPV calculations and how they align with Forbo's financial reporting needs. David shared insights into the current processes at Forbo and highlighted areas where automation could streamline operations. We also touched upon the potential integration of Zycus solutions to enhance procurement efficiency and cost management. Next Steps: • Send Over Documents: David will provide a sanitized version of the PPV and NPV bridge documents for your review. • Review Configuration Options: Varun and the team will assess industry-standard configurations that align with your requirements and revert with suggestions. • Schedule Follow-Up Meeting: We aim to reconvene in the next few days to address any questions and refine our proposal further based on your feedback.
opp.ais_why:
• Nov 2025 Avoma notes capture extensive 'use of AI in procurement' discussion with verbatim Zycus pitch elements: 'AI features run on a dedicated Azure OpenAI instance under SOC 2 controls', 'Contract management AI accelerates review and reduces legal support time', and 'AI-driven spend analysis highlights site-level and inflation variances'. Forbo's team engaged on these AI-specific technical and business points. Merlin and Merlin Intake are in scope alongside iContract/iRisk/iSource/iSupplier.
• Formal Evaluation, BD Engaged. 6 completed meetings Jul-Nov 2025 with full Forbo team (Gerben Flinkert, David Gallo, Abel van Dam) and Zycus solution architects. A 7-month implementation plan with Sept kickoff was discussed in the Nov 2025 session ‚Äî indicating active commercial momentum at that point. Last activity Jan 24, 2026 ‚Äî a 3-month gap since suggests recent cooling.
• High confidence: Avoma notes explicitly cover AI as a value differentiator with multiple specific points (Azure OpenAI, contract-AI, spend-analysis AI). Held at 7 because the conversation has been implementation/architecture-focused rather than AI-as-selection-criterion, and the LastActivityDate gap (Jan 2026 to May 2026) suggests deal may have cooled or moved to internal review.

contacts.name: Dam Abel Van
contacts.title: Supervisor SAP SAP Sales & Logistics Team & Product Owner E-commerce

items.quote: David is meeting with CFO to present business case

items.area: Identity of the second tender vendor unknown
items.quote: they will send out a tender but probably only to 2 vendors

metrics.narrative: The buyer articulated concrete operational pain and built a quantified case: on the 1 Aug 2025 PPV/NPV session David walked through Forbo's manual data-gathering effort and commodity-driven raw-material variance, and on the 7 Nov 2025 on-site he tied ROI directly to reduced legal support (with ~450 contracts to migrate into CLM) and to spend visibility once contracts are linked to SAP. A value case was sent to the CPO on 25 Aug 2025 and presented to the CFO on 26 Aug 2025, and budget was approved on 2 Oct 2025, so the financial case cleared finance. The pain is owned by procurement (David) with finance sign-off. The remaining gap is a re-confirmed, dated ROI figure for the tender response, which is a move rather than an unknown.

champion.narrative: David GALLO is a strong, active champion who has driven the deal end-to-end: built and presented the business case to CFO/CPO, secured budget approval (2 Oct 2025), coached Zycus on the two-vendor tender and Zycus's advantage (12 Oct 2025), and led every working session including the 7 Nov 2025 on-site. His advocacy is concrete: he shaped scope in Zycus's favour and openly stated the depth of engagement is Zycus's edge. What has changed is engagement cadence, not commitment: no deep working call since 7 Nov 2025, and his most recent touch is a light event attendance (~5 Jun 2026 Amsterdam roundtable). The gap is explained by the tender process and the S4HANA delay, but the champion has not been re-activated on the deal itself, so he reads active-but-cooling and needs a fresh working session.

competition.narrative: This is effectively a two-vendor tender in which Zycus is advantaged: David stated on 12 Oct 2025 the tender goes to only 2 vendors and Zycus has the edge from a year of engagement. The identity of the second vendor is not confirmed. Ivalua is the most likely peer, raised by David on 1 Aug 2025 ('a desire for a solution like Ivalua'); Coupa and SAP Ariba came up only as market references (Zycus's own rep is ex-Coupa; Ariba referenced for another customer), not as Forbo's shortlist. The wedge is purpose-built fit: Zycus's tender response must mirror every workshop output (phased go-live, iSaaS Lite integration, AI contract review, EU data residency, resourcing) so it reads bespoke while a rival's looks generic. The open risk is that a formal tender re-introduces price and feature-parity pressure.

identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is manual, tool-poor procurement: today Forbo runs sourcing and contracts on ERP reports, BI, Excel and email, cannot match vendor spend for compliance reporting ('if they put a dash instead of a comma you can't match the vendor'), and wants to professionalize and cut legal support time on contracts. David stated the ambition plainly on 7 Nov 2025: reduce legal involvement, capture savings quickly, and get spend coverage reporting once contracts are linked to the relevant stakeholder pain, implied by the SAP ECC to S4HANA migration and single-ERP standardization, is the risk of reinventing standard processes, which the buyer explicitly wants to avoid. Pain owner is David (procurement) with the Global Head of Procurement above him.

sources: Avoma call 7 Nov 2025 (Neeraj on SIT/UAT sign-off gates)
paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are partly visible. Commercially the buyer wants revised quotes for both lean and advanced integration models plus an IT-FTE resource plan (requested 12 Aug 2025), and on 7 Nov 2025 discussed whether the iSaaS choice and partner services sit inside one contract. A third-party implementation/project-management partner is expected: Forbo has no internal PM and asked Zycus to recommend partners who can run project and change management with Zycus as prime. InfoSec and data-residency were addressed on 7 Nov 2025 (Germany data centre, AWS backup, SOC 1 / SOC 2 Type 2). Formal legal/MSA/SOW steps and procurement channel are not yet documented; the tender itself will define the paper path, so its issuance is the forcing function.

economic_buyer.narrative: Budget authority is confirmed but the economic buyer is not named or personally engaged by the relevant stakeholder value case went to the CPO (25 Aug 2025) and David presented it to the CFO (26 Aug 2025), and budget was approved (2 Oct 2025), so an EB exists and has said yes to funding. On 13 Feb 2026 Caroline noted a call with the relevant stakeholder of Procurement, indicating a senior procurement owner above David. No Global Head of Procurement is on the Salesforce contact roles or on any recorded call, so Zycus has reached the EB only through David. Mapping and getting a direct line to that budget owner ahead of the April tender is the ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The process is a formal tender to only two vendors, with Zycus acknowledged as advantaged from 12 months of engagement (David, 12 Oct 2025). The path David laid out: engage an external consultant Jan 2026 to prep functional requirements, issue the tender (originally March, slipped to April 2026 behind the S4HANA project), select the platform mid-2026, kick off implementation September 2026, first modules live February 2027. The buyer runs procurement centrally from the division head office (central finance, IT, procurement, R&D), and the call sequence shows IT and SAP stakeholders joining the implementation-scoping stage, signalling the evaluation has moved from functional fit into integration and delivery diligence. The near-term gate is tender issuance, which is currently delayed.

decision_criteria.narrative: Criteria are well understood from the sessions: a procurement platform sitting above SAP that is near-standard (minimal costly customization), phased so Source-to-Contract, CLM and Spend can go live before full P2P/SAP integration, with flexible SAP ECC/CPI integration and a clear lean (iSaaS Lite) versus advanced integration choice. On 7 Nov 2025 the buyer leaned to iSaaS Lite given their single, standardized SAP ECC 6.0 landscape and stated a strong preference to avoid heavy adaptation ('if we make too much adaptation to the system we select we pay more'). AI value (dedicated Azure OpenAI, SOC 2 Type 2, contract-review acceleration, spend-variance analysis) and EU data residency (Germany primary, AWS backup) are explicit criteria. These are confirmed from the buyer's own words, not inferred.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're behind on this one — the reads below say why and what would change it.

bullets.full: Champion is driving it — David GALLO is a strong, active champion who built the business case, secured budget, and shaped the two-vendor tender in Zycus's favour. He is not at risk of switching, but he has not been re-engaged on a working session since 7 Nov 2025 and his latest touch is a light event attendance, so the relationship needs re-activation before the tender opens.

The pain is owned by procurement (David) with finance sign-off. The remaining gap is a re-confirmed, dated ROI figure for the tender response, which is a move rather than an unknown.

blocks.title: What moves it forward

cro_panel.header: Forbo Flooring Systems — Formal Evaluation · $250K · closes 31 Dec

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 14 is driven by Verdict 'Slowing' — momentum stalling. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 41 is carried by champion strength +1.00 (weight 15); business case strength +1.00 (weight 10). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 52 (roughly flat): Next Step carries 14 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (engagement, stage integrity, exec and champion, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 26 is anchored on the stage win (41), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 52, and dragged by risk 14. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

contributions.evidence: Next Step carries 14 dated milestone(s) — actively worked.

Close date moved 30 Jun 2026 to 31 Dec 2026

deal_movement.summary: Strong build through 2025 (business case to CFO Aug, budget approved Oct, deep IT/implementation on-site Nov) has flattened in 2026: confidence marked 35% and tender delayed to April behind S4HANA (Feb), close date pushed to 31 Dec 2026, and only a light event touch since. The gating tender has not issued.

reasons.detail: Forbo Flooring Systems is a 250k (is_large) opportunity in Formal Evaluation (win 40.6) with a strong champion (David GALLO) who shaped a two-vendor tender in Zycus's favour, but it has produced zero activity, movement, or call in the last 14 days and 31 days since any touch. The buyer's own tender - expected to start April 2026 (due 2026-04-30) - has still not surfaced and the deal is now drifting into July with no re-engagement of David since 7 Nov 2025. If left unwatched, a 250k deal built on 12 months of requirement shaping goes cold right before the competitive tender opens, letting Ivalua close the engagement gap.
reasons.metric: 31 days since last activity; 0 activities/calls/movements in 14 days; tender ~2 months overdue (expected Apr 2026)
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Caroline why a 250k Forbo deal has had no touch in 31 days, whether the April tender has actually launched, and when David GALLO was last re-engaged.
reasons.summary: 250k Forbo tender deal gone quiet 31 days; the April tender never opened.

confidence_signals.summary: Cooling: buyer-marked confidence 35% (Feb 2026), no deep working call since Nov 2025, tender delayed, and only a light event touch since. The advantaged position is intact but going quiet.

north_star_verdict.math: Days to close 181 (close 31 Dec 2026). Created 25 Jun 2025; still in Formal Evaluation ~12 months later. Buyer's own path per Next Step: tender ~April 2026 (delayed from March by S4HANA), platform selection ~summer 2026, kickoff Sept 2026, first modules live Feb 2027. Last deep buyer call 7 Nov 2025; last touch 5 Jun 2026 (David attended the Amsterdam roundtable, a light event touch, not deal progression). Selection-to-contract path points to a Q3-Q4 2026 commercial close; the 31 Dec date is credible on the calendar but the tender has not yet issued, so the gating action is stalled.
evidence: Confidence marked 35% on 13 Feb 2026 per Next Step

Budget approved 2 Oct 2025 per Next Step
Value case sent to CPO 25 Aug 2025; David meeting CFO 26 Aug 2025 per Next Step
Buyer stated 12 Oct 2025 the tender goes to only 2 vendors and Zycus has an advantage from depth of prior engagement
On-site IT/implementation session 7 Nov 2025 covered phased go-live, iSaaS Lite vs Advanced, SAP ECC/CPI integration, data residency and dedicated Azure OpenAI
David attended the Zycus Amsterdam AI World Tour roundtable ~5 Jun 2026 (event touch, per Task)
No deep buyer call since 7 Nov 2025 (~8 months)
north_star_verdict.headline: Zycus is one of only two invited tender vendors with acknowledged advantage after 12 months of deep engagement, but momentum has thinned: no deep buyer call since 7 Nov 2025, confidence marked 35% on 13 Feb 2026, and the tender slipped from March to April 2026 behind Forbo's S4HANA project.

flags: Re-activate David and confirm the April tender is live: 35% confidence and an 8-month working-call gap threaten the advantaged position before the formal round opens.
Reach the economic buyer directly (Global Head of Procurement / CFO): budget is approved but Zycus only knows the EB through David, leaving the deal single-threaded at power.
De-risk delivery by naming the implementation/PM partner Forbo needs: their lack of an internal project manager is the biggest non-competitive obstacle to a September kickoff.
best_practice_check.summary: Zycus is advantaged but cooling; the win now depends on re-activating the deal before the tender, locking the economic buyer, and de-risking delivery capacity.

competitive_position.summary: A two-vendor formal tender in which Zycus is the advantaged incumbent-of-engagement after 12 months of requirement shaping (David, 12 Oct 2025). Ivalua is the strongest current peer threat, named by David on 1 Aug 2025 as a desired solution class, though not confirmed as the second tender vendor. Coupa and SAP Ariba are dormant market references only (no buyer lean toward either). No new competitor evidence surfaced this sweep, so prior threat levels are carried forward unchanged; the live risk is not a preferred rival but price/feature parity being introduced by the formal tender itself.

competitors.how_we_win: Depth of engagement and requirements shaping over 12 months; make the tender response mirror every workshop output (phased go-live, iSaaS Lite, AI contract review, EU data residency) so Zycus reads purpose-built and the peer looks generic.

competitors.quote: The world that they use Ariba, remember?
competitors.source: Avoma call 1 Aug 2025, reference to another customer, not Forbo's shortlist
competitors.how_we_win: Not confirmed as shortlisted; treat as dormant. If it is the second vendor, lead with Zycus end-to-end integration (iSaaS) and native AI vs Ariba complexity.

The one exposure is delivery capacity, since Forbo has no internal project manager and depends on a Zycus-recommended partner.

12/08/2025: IT session

Revised quotations for both lean (ISAS lean) and advanced integration models were requested, accompanied by an updated Excel resource plan detailing IT FTE involvement.

Clarification was sought on API integrations and middleware support, including whether Zycus provides a plug‐and‐play install for SAP ECC and how master data is exchanged.

Questions were raised regarding Zycus' ERP integration expertise with SAP to avoid reinventing standardized processes and to understand potential digital access costs.

The impact of the SaaS model was discussed, specifically the three updates per year and whether these releases would affect ERP integration testing and require additional internal effort.

Next steps were agreed upon to provide updated quotes, resource planning for both integration models, and details on cost implications for additional systems like Pantheon within the week.

02/08/2025: Caroline shared a benefits deck to help David build the business Case for the CFO

01/08/2025: During our discussion, we explored the intricacies of the PPV and NPV calculations and how they align with Forbo's financial reporting needs. David shared insights into the current processes at Forbo and highlighted areas where automation could streamline operations. We also touched upon the potential integration of Zycus solutions to enhance procurement efficiency and cost management.

Next Steps:

• Send Over Documents: David will provide a sanitized version of the PPV and NPV bridge documents for your review.

• Review Configuration Options: Varun and the team will assess industry-standard configurations that align with your requirements and revert with suggestions.

• Schedule Follow-Up Meeting: We aim to reconvene in the next few days to address any questions and refine our proposal further based on your feedback.
